frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Gradual integration of sensory evidence over time to drive a decision.

frameworks.name: cortico-striatal decision circuit
frameworks.one_line: Striatum proposed as a direct accumulation node beyond cortical regions.

core_question: Which brain region directly implements evidence accumulation during perceptual decision-making?
key_assumptions: Accumulation regions must causally affect behavior, encode graded evidence, and contribute throughout accumulation.
Posterior parietal cortex and frontal orienting fields may not be the direct accumulators.
manipulations: Pharmacological inactivation of anterior dorsal striatum (ADS).
Optogenetic perturbation during the auditory accumulation task.
Electrophysiological recording across the trial timecourse.
key_findings: ADS neurons exhibit graded encoding of accumulated evidence.
ADS contributes throughout the accumulation period, not just at choice.
interpretations: ADS is the first identified region satisfying all three criteria for direct involvement in evidence accumulation.
ADS may be a primary node of the evidence-accumulation network.
